title: Musings of Faulthurst
author: Faulthurst
variants:
Some have asked me why the lands outside change so drastically between rooms.

The simple answer is because we trust our eyes in a place where we are fools to trust any of our senses.

The long answer?

Even if I had the long answer for you, I wouldn't trust my eyes to read it correctly. Much less yours.
All the stories you find here really happened. On this train. That doesn't mean they occurred in reality. Only that these stories undeniably happened. Just as we are undeniably witnesses to them.

Congratulations on seeing the Dungeon Train, traveler. Congratulations on seeing it and exploring it with your own two eyes. Congratulations on listening to it.

And while you may never smell it, take it from me, you're not really missing out.

The reality of the Dungeon Train can be very grim... But you can extract the pleasure of exploration from me just as easily as you can extract my beating heart from me. I would have the same chance of survival either way.

It's a pleasure beyond pleasures to lay witness to your adventure.

Do your best to survive.

If you don't, try to come back! I've seen it happen with others! How they do it, I haven't the foggiest.

I find you fascinating. Yes, you. Your differences. Everyone who's come here. The subtle ways in which you differentiate yourselves without even trying.

You could stand still for two hours just looking around and that would still manage to be... utterly unique.

It's fine to kill the slimes. No, honestly, please do, they're pests.

Do they live? I couldn't answer that. A tree lives. A tree is a beautiful, otherworldly pleasure to me, and I would cut it down happily. Had I the ability. Or upper body strength.

The iron maidens are as mysterious to me as they are to you, that I can promise. I am no omniscient being, I don't know how this started or where it will end, and the only difference between you and I is that I'm much harder to kill and I've had plenty of time to utilize that distinct quality of mine.

I still don't know what's up with the iron maidens, however.

The books will be opening soon. Not the books I've seen you adventurers carry around with you, those are magical books. Magical, at least, in the sense that I cannot explain how it is they work. I'm certain if I could explain it, it would then cease, very quickly, to be magical.

No, the regular books. They'll be opening soon.

The emeralds are tempting, are they not? A chest full of emeralds would also be extremely tempting, even out in the desert, where not even a million emeralds could buy you one drop of beautiful, life-sustaining water. Somehow the allure is hard to shake off.

That said, do continue collecting emeralds, they will come very much in handy. If not for you then for someone else.

Who do you think made this place? I've considered how the question resembles the question of God and the afterlife, but I mean literally. Who made this place? Someone had to have, right?

No, I don't mean, again, in the sense of the divine and of creationism. I mean brick by brick. Who designed it, who's hand guided the creation of — oh darn it! The questions are different! I'm just having a hard time expressing how.

I've seen quite a few people explore the dungeon train and not find anything close to what they're looking for. But you adventurers are different. I've stopped trying to keep count.

In some strange way, this wave of adventurers and explorers... I still don't know what to call you guys specifically... I feel as though the dungeon train's been waiting for you specifically. Maybe you'll find something on this darn train.

People die on here, yes. Adventurers too, yes. However, it never seems to bother the adventurers as much. Oh sure, I've seen annoyance. One person's screams were cut off during a flustered rage, it's true.

But... it wasn't dying rage. It wasn't... the snuffing of a candle. Simply anger. You people are just fascinating to me.

Somewhere in my heart of hearts, I know that time isn't supposed to move the way it does in the dungeon train. Another one of those things that can't be explained. Among many.

Days are supposed to be as long as nights, and both are supposed to be substantially longer than they are in here. I know that somehow.

Observation of the exterior world is supposed to inform internal insight. Not this time.

What are those red things inside the slimes, anyway? Hearts? Perhaps a nucleus?

I suppose I could study it to a rudimentary degree. I'm still trying to work up the guts to do it. I'm stalling, admittedly.

Why is it you think, there are so many peices of armour? And for that matter ... armour stands? Is there some armourer traversing these trains equipping it? Perhaps a great many of them! Or is these whole train just a vessel for transporting armour.

What is a train? A connected series of vehicles that travel along a railway track to transport passengers or freight. Is the definition tied ot the purpose? If the transportation is just a consquence, is it still a train?
 What is your purpose? Does it define who you are? Or are you just a passanger on this rollercoaster that could also be defined as a "train"?

I once found a sausage hidden deep in the depths of this train. Usually I find pork, beef, chicken, even rabbit. But a sausage? And only one? Who made it? 
 Everything on this train repeats itself eventually, never exactly the same of course, but never truely different either.
 But.. not the sausage. It was one of a kind.
What you ever considered the weight of an inifite train? Because each of these blocks would weight something constant. But there is seemlingly infinite of them.
Have you seen those carraiges just full of dirt, mud or ice? Sometimes they hide secrets... Treasures, if you will.
For a train, there is a surpising amount of pots on here. But I guess for a dungeon, not so suprising at all.
For an infinite train, I haven't seen many bathrooms. Makes you wonder...
Its not that I want to leave this train, because I'm sure if I did, I could, but yet... I dont.

Ah adventure, you have found my most secret of notes yet. Hidden here in this particural spot, for reasonsly on you or I can know. Only I wont tell you that part, you'll have to make sense of it yourself.
The passengers of this train dont say much. The odd adevnture is chatty, many leaving notes and a couple leaving a soft spot in my heart.

Adventurers tend to gravitate toward more opulent, decorative swords and weapons I've noticed. Not so with the other passengers of the train.

I wonder what the difference between you and them is.
unsued:
That book. I wish to ask questions about it, but it seems you're as able to tell me about them as you are able to let go of the darn things.

Do you know how they work? I don't. I'd like to. Would you like to? No?
